skill.name: tavily-search
skill.description: Search the web with LLM-optimized results via the Tavily CLI. Use this skill when the user wants to search the web, find articles, look up information, get recent news, discover sources, or says "search for", "find me", "look up", "what's the latest on", "find articles about", or needs current information from the internet. Returns relevant results with content snippets, relevance scores, and metadata — optimized for LLM consumption. Supports domain filtering, time ranges, and multiple search depths.
skill.summary: Web search returning LLM-optimized results with content snippets and relevance scores
skill.author: tavily-ai

security_audit.summary: All 53 static findings are false positives. The skill is a legitimate web search wrapper for the Tavily CLI. External command patterns are documented CLI usage examples restricted to the `tvly` binary only. Network access is limited to the official Tavily API. No arbitrary command execution, credential exfiltration, or malicious patterns detected. Risk level: SAFE.

content.user_title: Search the Web with LLM-Optimized Results
content.value_statement: Finding accurate, current information on the internet is time-consuming. This skill provides AI-ready search results optimized for LLM consumption with relevance scores, content snippets, and domain filtering.
actual_capabilities: Execute web searches via Tavily CLI with relevance-ranked results
Filter results by domain, date range, topic, and geographic region
Retrieve full page content and AI-generated answers alongside results
Support multiple search depths from ultra-fast to advanced precision
Output structured JSON optimized for AI processing
limitations: Requires Tavily API key and CLI installation on the system
Results depend on Tavily index coverage; some sources may be unavailable
Rate limits apply based on Tavily subscription tier

use_cases.title: Research and Fact-Checking
use_cases.description: Quickly gather current information on any topic with relevance-ranked sources and content snippets for verification.
use_cases.target_user: Developers, writers, and analysts who need accurate, cited information

use_cases.title: News Monitoring
use_cases.description: Track recent developments on specific topics, companies, or technologies with time-filtered news results.
use_cases.target_user: Product managers, journalists, and researchers monitoring industry trends

use_cases.title: Technical Documentation Search
use_cases.description: Find official documentation, API references, and technical articles from trusted domains.
use_cases.target_user: Engineers and developers building integrations or debugging issues

prompt_templates.title: Basic Web Search
prompt_templates.scenario: When user needs general information or current facts

prompt_templates.title: News Search with Date Filter
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for recent news about {topic} from the past week
prompt_templates.scenario: When user wants current developments on a topic

prompt_templates.title: Domain-Filtered Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for {topic} from official sources like example.com and developer.example.io
prompt_templates.scenario: When user needs authoritative or technical sources

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Research Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for {topic} using advanced depth, include full content and AI answers, max 10 results
prompt_templates.scenario: When user needs comprehensive research results

output_examples.input: Search the web for 'AI coding assistants 2025'
output_examples.output: Returns ranked results with title, URL, content snippet, and relevance score from recent sources

output_examples.input: Search for 'rust programming latest news' time-range week topic news
output_examples.output: Returns recent news articles about Rust programming with dates and source credibility indicators

best_practices: Use specific, focused queries under 400 characters for better results
Filter by trusted domains when researching technical topics
Choose appropriate search depth: basic for general use, advanced for precision tasks
anti_patterns: Avoid vague queries like 'tell me about technology' - be specific about the topic
Do not use this for real-time chat autocomplete - use ultra-fast depth only when latency is critical
Avoid excluding all domains if you need authoritative results - use include-domains instead

faq.question: How do I get started with tavily-search?
faq.answer: Install the Tavily CLI with curl -fsSL https://cli.tavily.com/install.sh | bash, then run tvly login with your API key. The skill will then execute searches on your behalf.

faq.question: What is the difference between search depths?
faq.answer: Ultra-fast is fastest with lower relevance, basic is the default balance, and advanced provides the highest relevance at slower speed for precision tasks.

faq.question: Can I search for content from specific websites?
faq.answer: Yes, use --include-domains to restrict results to specific domains like --include-domains github.com,stackoverflow.com.

faq.question: How do I get full article content in search results?
faq.answer: Add --include-raw-content to your search command to include full page content alongside the search results.

faq.question: What happens if my search query is too long?
faq.answer: Keep queries under 400 characters. For complex research, break it into multiple focused sub-queries.

faq.question: Is this skill suitable for real-time applications?
faq.answer: Use the ultra-fast depth for real-time needs like autocomplete. For research and fact-checking, use basic or advanced depth for better relevance.
